rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLEN J. CHUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/9/2025. This final account and report is not on waiver of account. The following issues to be cleared two weeks before the next hearing for the court's further review: (1) Petition paragraph #13 misstates the total credit amount. Summary of Account sets forth $4,125,653.27; however, the accurate amount seems $4,107,653.27. Please see the ending balance. (2) Notice must be directly sent to each person entitled to. C/O only appears insufficient. CRC 7.51(a)and(b). The trust (minor) beneficiaries to be noticed. The trustee of the memorial trust must be directly noticed, too. (3) Statutory compensation payable to attorney - the total fee base should be $4,546,964.95 not $4,546,182.18. with the discrepancy, the fee requested $9 less than the entitled amount. (4) Clarification of whether refunds have been added to the fee base. the amount seems to be paid by the existing estate funds. If so, this amount should not be included in the fee base. a) Refund totaling $5,507.69 for 13835 Francisco Dr. b) Refund totaling $3,664.64 for 1580 Lombard St. c) Refund totaling $2,257.40 for 33207 Kennedy St. d) Liquidating distribution totaling $38,460.96 - if they are the combination of principal and interest/dividends. (5) Further explanation re: erroneous deposits (from) Rony Gramajo for the total amount of $25,790 (Schedule A, page 80), including whether they were refunded or not. (6) Further allegation regarding disbursement of legal payment to Hanowsky Law for litigation as well as Daniel Conrad for litigation. Disbursement Schedule shows payment to them; however, petition is silent. (7) Clarification whether the personal rep was reimbursed for the out-of-pocket expenses for the WA property in the amount of $41,195.91 or Petition paragraph #14.b. merely reporting that the estate funds had to cover the ancillary administration fees as Disbursement Schedule does not show separate $41,195.91. (8) Prayer must list and describe in detail the property to be distributed. CRC 7.651. Proposed order must be revised accordingly.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUTH LUCAS PARDINI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have yet to be addressed: 1) The property on hand at beginning of account does not match the assets listed in the inventory and appraisal filed September 19, 2022, which include personal property appraised at $1,500. Please amend the accounting to include all property inventoried and appraised. 2) Shirley Yawitz filed a request for special notice on September 12, 2024, but does not appear to have been served with the petition or the supplement filed. Please provide Ms. Yawitz with sufficient notice of the instant petition. 3) Please clarify whether Ms. Yawitz was paid for her services as probate referee. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SUNNY NG MITCHELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Enforcing Power Of Attorney And For Fees Against Chase Bank For Refusal To Honor Power Of Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/24/2025, as the following issues (see tentative rulings for 09/11/2024 and 01/15/2025 hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) No proof of service on Tweedy Browne as the remaining respondent is on file. The respondent must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 4544(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. applicable to a civil summons. 4) The principal's current address must be provided by a verified allegation. 5) Has the petitioner furnished an affidavit under section 4305 to the respondent? If so, please file a copy. 6) Did the principal own the Tweedy Browne account when the principal's trust was created in December 1999? If so, did the trust instrument (including its schedule, attachment, or exhibit) or general assignment of property (if any) confirm the account as an asset of the trust?

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: LUCAS ANTHONY ARDUINI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/2025. The petition does not provide sufficient material for the Court to establish the fact related the death. Especially, Form BMD-003A, DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT, TIME, AND PLACE OF DEATH and other attachments, if applicable, not on file. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
